title: Loxogramme lankokiensis
taxonomy: Loxogramme lankokiensis is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. It belongs to the genus Loxogramme, characterized by their distinctive leaf shapes. The species Lankokiensis is a species of fern that is native to Sri Lanka. Loxogramme lankokiensis is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.

intro: Loxogramme lankokiensis, also known as Lankoki fern, is a species of fern in the family Loxogrammaceae, characterized by its unique, delicate fronds. The Lankoki fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Loxogramme lankokiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lankoki fern include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
